iterations.theme: The Significance of St. Francis' Relics, Divine Providence, and Trust in God's Plan
iterations.reason_for_cut: The entire transcript is shorter than the minimum required length for a single iteration (35-45 minutes), so it is presented as one complete thematic unit covering the whole discussion.

chapters.topic: Arrival of St. Francis' relics and the meaning of veneration
Relics as a sign of hope and proof that a Gospel-centered life is possible
Addressing discouragement and feeling stuck in one's spiritual journey
St. Francis as the 'poor one' – not materially, but by stripping away ego
Human tendency to dictate terms and scenarios to God
Introduction to St. Paul's question: 'Who has bewitched you?'
Explanation of 'bewitched' and the importance of 'disillusionment' (seeing reality)
Critique of trusting in one's own righteousness vs. trusting in Jesus
The role of wounds in salvation and learning life lessons
Healing wounds and the purpose of scars as reminders
The importance of opening oneself to Divine Providence
Understanding Divine Providence as God being 'my Dad' (Abba)
Refuting the idea that God has 'more important things' than our daily lives
God is on our team, not a neutral judge, and cares most for the struggling
St. Francis' dramatic act of surrendering to God as his heavenly Father
Practicing 'evangelical impudence' – sharing everything with God in prayer
St. Francis' world-changing impact through openness to Divine Providence
